name: parallelization
summary: Fan out the same or related subtasks to parallel agents, then merge. Two variants: sectioning (different pieces) and voting (same piece, multiple opinions).
when_to_use: Subtasks are independent (sectioning) or you want multiple perspectives on the same question (voting). Fast; parallelism dominates serial cost.

layers.skill: skills/council-review (in agent-teams skill) covers council patterns; skills/agent-teams topology table.
layers.hook: Optional: Stop hook that waits for all parallel agents to complete before closing the turn.
layers.agent: agents/council-coordinator orchestrates fan-out/fan-in with structured merge.

cost: Wall-clock: ~1×. Total tokens: N× where N = parallel count. Compare to serial: same tokens, N× slower.
anti_patterns: Parallelizing tasks that share mutable state — merge conflicts and hallucinated consistency.
No merge protocol — each agent reports separately; user has to synthesize.
Voting with fewer than 3 agents — not a vote, just a chain.
